title: The Midnight Arcade - Part I: The Glitch
setting: 1986. A small, quiet town in the Pacific Northwest. An abandoned arcade at 2 AM.

characters.name: The Friend
characters.description: An urban explorer and childhood friend of the missing boy, Toby. Armed only with a BMX and a sense of mounting dread.

items.name: Rusted Pry Bar
items.description: Heavy, cold, and caked in decades of red earth. It'll open anything that isn't bolted to reality.

items.name: Silver Pendant
items.description: A tarnished 'T' on a thin chain. It's Toby's. It's still warm, as if it was just dropped.

items.name: Master Keycard
items.description: A yellowed plastic rectangle with a magnetic strip. STARLIGHT ARCADE: AUTHORIZED PERSONNEL ONLY.

items.name: Solder Gun
items.description: Old industrial model. The tip is still hot. Someone was working here—recently.

items.name: The Red Quarter
items.description: A single 1982 Washington quarter, pulsing with a dull, internal red light. It hums a low, synthetic bass note.

scenes.name: Arcade Exterior
scenes.prose: The Starlight Arcade is a jagged tooth of cracked cement and neon tubes. The 'S' and 'T' are dark, leaving only 'A-R-L-I-G-H-T' to flicker against the bruised purple sky. Your BMX bike leans against a rusted light post, its chrome frame reflecting the stuttering glow. A heavy iron side-entrance is locked tight, but a rusted-pry-bar lies in the bed of a nearby pickup, glinting under the moon. The air is still, smelling of pine and the ozone of an oncoming storm. Somewhere deep inside, a machine is humming.

lookDescriptions.bmx-bike: Your only ticket out of this town. It looks small against the massive, silent arcade.
lookDescriptions.side-door: Heavy steel, bolted shut. The locks are internal.
lookDescriptions.pickup-truck: An old Ford, caked in mud. A rusted-pry-bar is lying among some loose gravel in the bed.

scenes.name: The Lobby
scenes.prose: The lobby is a cathedral of stale popcorn and silence. Glass-topped prize counters sit empty, their dusty shelves holding only the ghosts of plastic rings and stuffed bears. To the north, the main floor stretches into darkness, rows of dormant machines standing like sentinels. A jagged hole in a display case catches the light—a silver-pendant hangs from a shard of glass, swaying gently in a draft that shouldn't exist. The air tastes of dry attic dust and copper.

lookDescriptions.counters: Empty and caked in dust. Fingerprints mar the surface—recent, and small.
lookDescriptions.glass-shards: A display case was smashed from the inside out.
lookDescriptions.main-floor: Darkness and the silhouettes of a hundred arcade cabinets.

scenes.name: The Main Floor
scenes.prose: The main floor is a labyrinth of dead screens and joystick shadows. Hundreds of cabinets stand silent, their wood-grain sides peeling like old skin. But at the center, one machine pulses with a soft blue light—*THE VOID*. Its marquee is blank, its joystick missing, but its coin slot glows with anticipation. To the west, a door is propped open with a chair, leading to a repair-bay. To the east, a heavy iron gate blocks the way down to a basement.

lookDescriptions.void-machine: The only machine with power. Its screen is a deep, rippling blue.
lookDescriptions.iron-gate: A heavy gate controlled by an old magnetic-card-reader.
lookDescriptions.repair-door: Propped open with a busted stool. Faint yellow light spills from within.

scenes.name: Repair Bay
scenes.prose: The repair bay is a graveyard of circuit boards and gutted monitors. A single desk lamp casts long, twitching shadows. Workbenches are cluttered with piles of arcade-tokens and tangles of wire. A solder-gun sits on a ceramic stand, its tip still glowing a faint orange. To the north, a heavy oak door marked 'MANAGER' is sealed with an electronic lock. The hum here is louder—not from the machines, but from the walls themselves.

lookDescriptions.workbench: Covered in schematic diagrams for 'THE VOID'. They look... organic.
lookDescriptions.manager-door: The keypad is dark. It needs a massive surge to bypass.
lookDescriptions.soldering-iron: Still hot. Someone was in a hurry.

scenes.name: Basement Entry
scenes.prose: A steep flight of concrete stairs descends into a damp, lightless void. The walls are caked in a black, root-like growth that pulses in time with your heartbeat. A massive server-rack stands at the top of the stairs, its cables tangled like intestines. The air down here is thick, freezing, and smells of wet ozone. There are no lights below—only the feeling of a vast, open space waiting for you.

lookDescriptions.stairs: They go deep. The 'roots' on the walls seem to be growing from the bottom up.
lookDescriptions.server-rack: The brain of the building. The wiring is a mess of shorts and bypasses.
lookDescriptions.black-growth: Slick and cold. It's not moss. It feels like carbon.

scenes.name: Manager Office
scenes.prose: The office is a time capsule of 1984. A heavy oak desk sits in the center, buried under ledger books and cigarette trays. On the wall, a bank of CRT security monitors flickers—though the cameras they're connected to have been gone for decades, they show the arcade floor as it was: bright, crowded, and full of life. Inside a locked drawer, a single red-quarter sits on a velvet cushion, glowing with a dull, internal heat. The air is thick with the smell of tobacco and cherry soda.

lookDescriptions.security-monitors: They're showing the past. You can see Toby's bike parked out front.
lookDescriptions.desk: Covered in receipts for 'The Void' cabinets. All signed by someone named 'VOX'.
lookDescriptions.red-coin: It's the only quarter in the room. It feels... heavy with purpose.

puzzles.result: The door's latch snaps with a gunshot crack. You pull the steel door open and slip into the stale air of the lobby.
You jam the silver pendant into Toby's favorite machine. The screen ripples. A hidden compartment in the cabinet's base clicks open.
The reader beeps. The heavy iron gate groans aside, revealing the dark stairs leading down.
You bridge two points on the server rack with the hot tip. A surge of sparks flies, and a heavy 'clack' echoes from the repair-bay—the manager's door is unlocked.
You insert the glowing quarter. The cabinet lets out a digital scream, and the glass screen dissolves into a shimmering portal. You step through, leaving the world behind.

winCondition.description: You step through the portal and into the screen. The physical world vanishes, replaced by a horizon of neon lines and pixelated clouds. TO BE CONTINUED IN PART II.
